---
name: dbd-migrate-to-neuron-protos
description: >
  Use this skill when a service still consumes the legacy product-level protobuf package —
  e.g. a Go import of "internal.os.alis.services/protobuf/alis/os/mcp/v1" or a go.mod require
  of "internal.<product>.<domain>/protobuf" — and should migrate to the per-neuron
  "alis.build" packages generated by protos.v2. Triggers: "migrate to neuron protos",
  "migrate to alis.build packages", the migration hint printed by the CLI/TUI after
  `alis packages upgrade` or the 'u' key, or build failures resolving the legacy module.
  Go-first: rewrites go.mod requires and import statements; JavaScript and Python are not
  yet automated. It changes only package plumbing — no business logic, no Define step.
metadata:
  alis.context.version: "1"
  # Context field paths this skill needs from the injected runtime context.
  alis.context.requires: >-
    focus_package_id workstations
---

# Migrate to per-neuron proto packages

Move a service off the legacy **product-level** protobuf package
(`internal.<product>.<domain>/protobuf` — one monolithic module bundling *all* of a product's
generated stubs, versioned by a build counter like `v1.945.1739`) onto the **per-neuron**
packages under the `alis.build` scope (one real-semver module per neuron, generated by
protos.v2). The generated types, proto packages, and call sites are identical — only the
module/import plumbing changes.

## When to use

- A manifest still requires the legacy `internal.<product>.<domain>/protobuf` module.
- The CLI/TUI printed the migration hint after `alis packages upgrade` / pressing `u`.
- You want neuron-level upgrades: bump one service's contract without pulling every other
  neuron's latest stubs.

## When not to use

| Need                                                       | Use instead                |
| ---------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------- |
| Author new protos for a service                            | **dbd-add-protos**         |
| Implement a Go gRPC server against an existing contract    | **dbd-add-grpc-go-server** |
| Just upgrade already-migrated packages                     | `alis packages upgrade`    |

## Runtime Context

This skill may be loaded with an `<alis-runtime-context>` block injected at the top of these
instructions. **When the block is present, its values are authoritative.**

| Value                 | Context field      | If absent, how to obtain it                                                       |
| --------------------- | ------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Service package ID**| `focus_package_id` | The dotted package ID of the service being migrated, e.g. `alis.os.mcp.v1`. Otherwise derive from the service folder (`~/alis.build/<org>/build/<product>/<service>/<vN>`) or ask the user. |
| **Service folder**    | `workstations`     | The focused workstation's build repo; otherwise `~/alis.build/<org>/build/<product>/<service>/<vN>`. |

## The mapping rule

A legacy import names the full neuron path inside the monolithic module:

```
internal.<product>.<domain>/protobuf/<org>/<product>/<neuron-path>/<vN>
```

The per-neuron module is:

```
alis.build/<org>/<product>/<neuron-path>[/<vN>]     # /v1 is trimmed; /v2+ is kept
```

and the **import path is the module root** — no subpath. Examples:

| Legacy import                                              | New import                    |
| ---------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------ |
| `internal.os.alis.services/protobuf/alis/os/mcp/v1`        | `alis.build/alis/os/mcp`       |
| `internal.os.alis.services/protobuf/alis/os/iam/v2`        | `alis.build/alis/os/iam/v2`    |
| `internal.os.alis.services/protobuf/alis/os/protos/golang/v1` | `alis.build/alis/os/protos/golang` |

Notes:

- The proto `package` (and therefore all generated type names) is unchanged. **Keep the
  existing import alias** (`pb "…"` stays `pb "…"`) so no call site changes.
- Cross-product legacy imports (`internal.<otherproduct>.<domain>/protobuf/...`) map by the
  same rule; they resolve through the accessible products' `define-go` registries.
- Do not touch `open.alis.services/protobuf` / `open.standards.exchange/protobuf` imports
  (open/standards protos) or `go.alis.build/*` helper libraries — they are not part of this
  scheme.
- Prerequisite: the neuron's per-neuron package must exist (its product has run Define through
  protos.v2). If `go get` later reports the module is unknown, the neuron has not been defined
  under the new scheme yet — stop and tell the user; do not invent a module path.

## Go procedure

1. **Inventory the legacy imports** in the service folder:

   ```bash
   grep -rEn 'internal\.[a-z0-9]+\.[a-z.]+/protobuf/' --include='*.go' .
   ```

   Collect the distinct `<org>/<product>/<neuron-path>/<vN>` suffixes.

2. **Rewrite every import** using the mapping rule, keeping aliases. E.g.

   ```go
   pb "internal.os.alis.services/protobuf/alis/os/mcp/v1"
   ```

   becomes

   ```go
   pb "alis.build/alis/os/mcp"
   ```

3. **Add the new requires** — one per distinct neuron. Prefer the CLI, which supplies the
   correct `GOPROXY`/`GONOSUMDB` and runs `go mod tidy` for you:

   ```bash
   alis packages install <service-package-id>
   ```

   Raw alternative (env values come from the `install_packages` command the CLI prints):

   ```bash
   GOPROXY="<proxy>" GONOSUMDB="<nosumdb>" go get alis.build/<org>/<product>/<neuron>@latest
   ```

4. **Drop the legacy require**: once no `internal.<product>.<domain>/protobuf/...` imports
   remain, `go mod tidy` (via `alis packages install`) removes the legacy require on its own.
   If any other product's legacy module is still imported elsewhere in the service, it stays —
   that is fine; migrate per neuron at your own pace.

## Verify

1. `go build ./...` and `go vet ./...` pass.
2. `alis packages upgrade <service-package-id>` now prints a scoped
   `go get -u alis.build/...` command **without** the legacy-package migration hint.
3. If the service has a playground (`.playground/main_test.go`), run it against DEV.

## JavaScript (not yet automated)

Target package: `@alis.build/<org>-<product>-<neuron-dashed>` (version segment kept, e.g.
`@alis.build/alis-os-mcp-v1`), served from the product's `define-ecmascript` Artifact Registry
repository. Manual steps mirror Go: replace `@internal.<product>.<domain>/protobuf` imports,
`pnpm add @alis.build/<...>@latest`, drop the legacy dependency.

## Python (not yet automated)

Target package: `<org>.<product>.<neuron>.<vN>` verbatim (pip normalizes to
`<org>-<product>-<neuron>-v<N>`), served from the product's `define-python` Artifact Registry
repository. Manual steps mirror Go: swap the requirement, update imports to the new package
root, remove the legacy `<domain>-protobuf-internal-<product>` requirement.
